guangzhou.name: Guangzhou
guangzhou.tagline: Eat Your Way Through Old Canton
guangzhou.best_time: October–December. Avoid Golden Week (Oct 1–7) and Chinese New Year.

attractions.name: Canton Tower
attractions.tip: Go at sunset — the light show starts after dark. Take the Bubble Tram (highest Ferris wheel in the world) for unique shots. Pair immediately with Pearl River cruise. Book timed entry online.
attractions.elderly_note: Elevator access throughout. No steps required.

attractions.name: Shamian Island
attractions.tip: 150+ European colonial buildings on a 0.3km² island. The mint-green Starbucks at No.54 Shamian Street is a former British consulate — unique photo spot. Go before 8am for golden light through banyan trees. Combine with Qingping Market nearby.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat, shaded streets. Ideal for elderly.

attractions.name: Chen Clan Ancestral Hall
attractions.tip: Guangdong's crown jewel of Lingnan architecture — triple roof sculptures, wood carvings, brick reliefs so intricate poet Guo Moruo wrote: 'Man can replace heaven's work, heaven cannot replicate man's.' 2025 update: AR technology recreates Qing dynasty ceremonies inside. Go morning.
attractions.elderly_note: Mostly flat with some steps. Worth the effort.

attractions.name: Yongqingfang & Enning Road
attractions.tip: The full Yongqingfang Tourism Zone includes Lychee Bay waterway, Cantonese Opera Art Museum, and Bruce Lee's ancestral home. The Guangcai ceramic painting workshop lets you paint porcelain for ¥98/hr. Monthly 2nd Saturday: spontaneous Cantonese Opera flash performance at Jinshen Plaza. Bridge light reflection at night looks like a crescent moon — come back after dark.
attractions.elderly_note: Some cobblestone paths. Rest spots available.

attractions.name: Dongshankou
attractions.tip: Called Guangzhou's Seoul Seongsu district — trendy Chinese fashion brands with unique aesthetics, indie roastery cafes, streetwear stores. 翠叶道 (Cuiye Road) nearby is a quiet stretch of cafes perfect for a break. Weekday afternoons best.
attractions.elderly_note: Moderate walking on flat streets.

attractions.name: Beijing Road Pedestrian Street
attractions.tip: 2025 upgrade: The '千年城脉博物馆' on B2 level beneath Guangbai Department Store reveals 11 layers of road surfaces from Qin dynasty to Republic era under glass. Walk in, look down — you're walking on history. Blind box toy shops everywhere. Evening buzz is electric.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat pedestrian street. Easy access.

attractions.name: Baoye Road Night Supper Street
attractions.tip: GZ's late-night supper (夜宵) scene. Grilled seafood, BBQ skewers, claypot congee. Comes alive after 10pm — eat like the locals eat at midnight. Order Chaoshan fish congee at Yong Zuo — thick, savory, absolutely local.
attractions.elderly_note: Noisy and crowded after 10pm. Consider earlier dinner alternatives.

attractions.name: Baiyun Mountain
attractions.tip: 2025 new: Star observation deck added at Moxing Peak summit — astronomy talks every Friday night. Cable car up, hike down for best experience. Go weekday morning for quiet. White Cloud Mountain: the 'First Mountain in the South.'
attractions.elderly_note: Cable car available. Not suitable for those with mobility issues on hike route.

attractions.name: Pearl River Night Cruise
attractions.tip: Pro tip: Skip the tourist cruise and take the 水上巴士 (water bus line) for just ¥2 — same river views, completely local experience. Jiangwan Ferry Pier has the best departure time at sunset. Depart from Tianzi Wharf for classic tourist version.
attractions.elderly_note: Fully accessible on cruise boats.

attractions.name: Yuexiu Park
attractions.tip: Largest urban park in downtown GZ. Five Rams Statue is Guangzhou's city symbol. Zhenhai Tower (600 years old) gives a surprisingly good city overview. Go 6–8am — locals do tai chi, ballroom dancing, singing. The real Guangzhou morning ritual.
attractions.elderly_note: Fully flat park. Perfect for elderly morning walks.

attractions.name: Museum of the Nanyue King
attractions.tip: Built directly over a 2,200-year-old royal tomb. Walk down into actual underground burial chambers — a jade burial suit with 2,291 pieces sewn in gold wire. One of China's top 100 archaeological discoveries. Nationally ranked first-class museum. Book via WeChat. Closed Mondays.
attractions.elderly_note: Ramp access to underground chambers. Suitable with assistance.

attractions.name: Sacred Heart Cathedral
attractions.tip: One of only 4 all-stone Gothic cathedrals in the world. 2025: Candlelit concerts every Wednesday and Saturday evening at 7pm. New craft market on the east side of the cathedral plaza sells hand-painted glass window fridge magnets — great souvenir.
attractions.elderly_note: Steps at entrance. Interior flat.

attractions.name: Shangxiajiu Pedestrian Street
attractions.tip: China's first commercial pedestrian street. Lined with 骑楼 (arcade-style colonial buildings). Best for food — century-old restaurants Guangzhou Restaurant (1935, '食在广州第一家') and Taotaoju on this strip. Also Lianxiang Lou for lotus paste cakes since 1889.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat street. Covered arcades provide shade.

attractions.name: Redtory Art & Design Factory
attractions.tip: Former canned food factory turned contemporary art campus. Galleries, studios, events, bookshops, coffee. 2025 address: 128 Yuancun 4th Cross Road. Less touristy than OCT Loft in Shenzhen — this one feels genuinely underground.
attractions.elderly_note: Mostly flat. Some uneven surfaces between buildings.

attractions.name: Huaisheng Mosque (Lighthouse Mosque)
attractions.tip: Built in 627 AD during the Tang Dynasty — China's oldest mosque, older than Islam's arrival in most of China. The 36m minaret once guided ships up the Pearl River as a literal lighthouse. Non-Muslims cannot enter prayer hall but can view courtyard and lighthouse tower. Dress modestly; scarves provided.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat courtyard. Suitable.

attractions.name: Ersha Island & Guangdong Museum of Art
attractions.tip: A quiet island in the Pearl River favored by locals. The 2025 new outdoor camping base lets you do picnics with Cantonese dim sum baskets. Good sunset views of Canton Tower from the western shore. Guangdong Museum of Art is free and genuinely excellent.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat island. Easy cycling available.

attractions.name: Hope & Sesame Speakeasy Bar
attractions.tip: Asia's 50 Best Bars winner since 2019. Hidden entrance inside a traditional Cantonese sundry shop — you have to find the secret door. Jazz every night. Open 6pm–2am daily. Book early on weekends — fills up completely. One of Guangzhou's most unique experiences.
attractions.elderly_note: Not suitable — bar environment, late hours.

attractions.name: Chimelong Tourist Resort
attractions.tip: Multiple parks: Safari World (world's largest, home to panda triplets 萌萌帅帅酷酷), International Circus (world's largest performance stage), Water Park (opens April 26 2025), Happy World. Kids and families: full day minimum. Featured in '爸爸去哪儿' TV show — enormous brand recognition in China.
attractions.elderly_note: Tram transport within resort. Suitable for elderly with mobility.

attractions.name: Liwan Lake Park
attractions.tip: One kilometre from Yongqingfang — quieter and more local. Former site of a Qing dynasty private garden. Morning: elderly locals singing, playing instruments, practicing calligraphy by the lake. Very peaceful contrast to the busy tourist areas nearby.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat park. Perfect for elderly.

attractions.name: Guangzhou Opera House
attractions.tip: Designed by Zaha Hadid — exterior looks like a pebble. Free to photograph outside. Best viewed from across Huacheng Square at night when lit. Pair with Canton Tower visit — both are in Zhujiang New Town. 2025: outdoor camping base opened at Ersha Island nearby.
attractions.elderly_note: Exterior only. Flat surroundings.

attractions.name: Guangxiao Temple
attractions.tip: One of Guangdong's oldest Buddhist temples — where the 6th patriarch of Zen Buddhism, Huineng, trained in 7th century. A pilgrimage site for Zen Buddhists worldwide. Much less touristy than Chen Clan but equally significant. Vegetarian restaurant inside: ¥10 meal admission if you eat there.
attractions.elderly_note: Mostly flat temple grounds.

attractions.name: Thirteen Hongs Museum
attractions.tip: The story of how Guangzhou was the ONLY port China opened to the West for 80+ years — and how the Thirteen Hongs merchant consortium became some of the world's wealthiest people. Surprisingly engaging for history lovers. Excellent English signage.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat museum. Suitable.

attractions.name: Huacheng Square (Flower City Square)
attractions.tip: Guangzhou's modern heart. '珠江之眼' (Eye of the Pearl River) observation deck lights up every night at 7:30pm. Fountain shows, art installations, families flying kites. Pairs well with Canton Tower and Opera House — all within walking distance.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat plaza. Ideal for elderly.

attractions.name: Sanjie Temple Area & Xiguan Old Houses
attractions.tip: West of Yongqingfang: the old Xiguan neighborhoods have traditional West Guangzhou mansion architecture (西关大屋) — screen doors with 满洲窗 (Manchurian stained glass), grand courtyards. Walk between Changshou West Road and Enning Road for the best examples. Almost no tourists.
attractions.elderly_note: Some narrow alleys. Mostly flat.

attractions.name: Panxi Restaurant Garden Teahouse
attractions.tip: Founded in 1947 — one of Guangzhou's four famous garden teahouses. Lingnan garden architecture with pavilions, koi ponds, moon gates. Order traditional dim sum in a setting that feels like the 1960s Guangzhou high life. Weekend mornings fill fast — book ahead.
attractions.elderly_note: Garden paths with some steps. Mostly accessible.

food.name: Dim Sum (Early Morning Tea)
food.where: Dim Dou Dak 点都德 (all-day including dinner, multiple locations); Taotaoju 陶陶居 (Shangxiajiu, est. 1880); Guangzhou Restaurant 广州酒家 (est. 1935, '食在广州第一家')
food.tip: Order: hongchangfen (脆皮肠粉 — rice noodle roll with crispy pastry inside shrimp), har gow (虾饺), cheong fun (猪肠粉), egg tarts. At Taotaoju: get the liu sha bao (流沙包, salted egg custard bun) — they invented it here. Check Dianping for ¥80/¥100 vouchers.

food.name: Crab Roe Noodles
food.where: Xie San Bao 蟹叁宝 — multiple locations
food.tip: Blew up on 小红书 and Lemon8 SG. Rich orange crab roe sauce coating fresh noodles. Distinctly seasonal flavor. Arrive 15 mins before opening to beat queues.

food.name: Char Siu BBQ Pork & Roast Meats
food.where: Any 烧腊 shop — look for hanging roast meat in the window. No specific chain — local shops are always better.
food.tip: The caramelised char siu glaze should have slightly charred edges — that's quality. Order with white rice (白饭). Get the combination platter: char siu + roast pork (烧肉) + roast duck (烧鸭). Under ¥40 for a full meal.

food.name: Claypot Rice
food.where: Wan Xing Claypot 万兴煲仔饭 — opens 5:30pm only. One chef, 15 stoves, queues before opening.
food.tip: Order frog or Chinese sausage & lap cheong version. The crispy rice crust (锅巴) at the bottom is the prized part — scrape and eat it. This is why locals queue.

food.name: Double Boiled Milk Pudding
food.where: Guangzhou Dessert institution on Baohua Road (宝华路); Shawan Sweet Shop 沙湾甜品食馆 on Xihua Road
food.tip: Made with buffalo milk — non-negotiable GZ order. Also try: 姜撞奶 (ginger milk pudding — poured hot milk into ginger juice to set), 凤凰炒奶 (pan-fried milk — unique texture), 芝麻糊 (hand-ground sesame paste — get the one with the 40-year old stone mill).

food.name: Chen Tianji Fish Skin & Pig Intestine Rice Noodle
food.where: 陈添记 — Baohua Road No.59, Liwan District (宝华路59号侧十五甫三巷). Est. 1937. Only sells 3 items.
food.tip: Only sells three things: chilled dace fish skin (爽鱼皮) with sesame & peanuts, pig intestine rice noodle (猪肠粉), and boat congee (艇仔粥). The fish skin is their signature — cold, bouncy, dressed in soy sauce and sesame. A local institution for 87 years.

food.name: Wonton Noodles
food.where: Baohua Noodle Shop 宝华面店 on Baohua Road; Hanming Noodles nearby
food.tip: GZ wonton noodles are different from HK — thinner 竹升面 (bamboo-pressed) noodles, smaller wontons. Baohua's broth uses roast pork bones, dried flounder, dried scallops, and fresh shrimp — the traditional 上汤 method. Can't get this anywhere else.

food.name: Boat Congee
food.where: Xihua Road food stalls; Pantang area near Liwan Lake; Panxi Restaurant
food.tip: Named after Pearl River boat dwellers who sold congee from sampans. Silky GZ-style congee base with fish slices, squid, peanuts, char siu, fried dough. Very different from congee elsewhere. Perfect breakfast dish.

food.name: Jasmine Milk Tea with Cheese Cream
food.where: Chali Tea 茶里 — Guangzhou-origin brand. Multiple locations.
food.tip: Blew up on 小红书 and Lemon8 among SEA travellers. Cheese cream layer on jasmine tea. Also try 阿嬷手作 (Grandma Handmade) for their rice wine fermented milk tea — completely unique to the PRD region.

food.name: Roasted Goose
food.where: Bingsheng Pinwei 炳胜品味 (Tianhe area); or any established 烧腊 restaurant displaying 烧鹅
food.tip: Guangzhou roast goose is the original — crispier skin than Peking duck, deeper flavour. Order half goose for 2–3 people. Dip in the plum sauce. This is what Cantonese food writers rank as the dish that best represents the region.

food.name: Zhu Ji Jiu (Pig's Trotter in Ginger & Vinegar)
food.where: Xihua Road stalls; Liwan district sweet shops
food.tip: A Cantonese postpartum tradition — sweet black vinegar braised pork trotter with ginger and boiled eggs. Sounds unusual but tastes incredible. Rich, tangy, warming. Served year-round in Guangzhou. Order with a bowl of congee.

food.name: Jidi Congee (Offal Congee)
food.where: Wuzhanji 伍湛记 — Longdong East Road & Longdong Middle Road, Liwan
food.tip: Pork balls, pork liver, pork intestine in silky congee — the 'three grades' of imperial exam (三元及第). Wuzhanji has been making this since the 1950s. Won China's National Famous Snack award in 1997. Find the shops by the queue.

food.name: White Cut Chicken
food.where: Any Cantonese restaurant — or Bingsheng Pinwei for premium version
food.tip: GZ's most famous dish to outsiders — gently poached free-range chicken, just-cooked through, served cold with ginger scallion oil. The secret is the chicken quality and the dipping sauce. Locals say: if the restaurant's white cut chicken is good, everything is good.

food.name: Taro Milk Tea (大芋头专线)
food.where: Trending at various milk tea shops in Tianhe and Dongshankou areas
food.tip: Chunky taro paste milk tea with real taro pieces — not the purple powder version. GZ's taro milk tea scene is much more artisanal than Singapore's. Look for handwritten menus and long local queues.

food.name: Coconut Ice Cream
food.where: Shunji Ice Room 顺记冰室 — Baohua Road No.85. Est. 1920s, one of Guangzhou's last four original ice parlors.
food.tip: Hand-made coconut ice cream using the original 1920s recipe. Very fresh, not over-sweet. The mango gelato is also excellent. A childhood memory for all Guangzhou locals over 40.

practical.payment: Set up Alipay International before landing. Link Visa/Mastercard via 'International Version.' WeChat Pay also works. Keep ¥200 cash for small stalls and markets.
practical.transport: Metro is clean, cheap, fully bilingual. Download Gaode Maps (高德地图) — far better than Google Maps for China. APM line (driverless monorail) is unique — sit in front or rear car for best views.
practical.connectivity: Get Airalo eSIM before flying or buy mainland SIM at airport. VPN essential — install before arriving. Google, Instagram, WhatsApp all blocked without VPN.
practical.language: Less English than Shanghai. Screenshot restaurant names in Chinese before going. Use Alipay's translation lens in restaurants.
practical.booking_apps: Dianping (大众点评) for restaurant vouchers — always cheaper than walk-in. Klook for Canton Tower tickets. Meituan for food delivery if staying multiple nights.
practical.book_ahead: Canton Tower timed entry (buy online). Popular dim sum on weekends — arrive before 10am or wait 45 mins. Panxi Restaurant — reservations recommended.
practical.best_time: October–December. Spring (March–April) also good. Summer is extremely hot and humid. Avoid Golden Week holidays.
practical.transport_tip: Three-day metro pass (¥50) covers 90% of attractions. Water bus ticket (¥2) for Pearl River views.

shenzhen.name: Shenzhen
shenzhen.tagline: China's Future City With a Local Soul

attractions.name: Ping An Finance Centre Observation Deck
attractions.chinese: 平安国际金融中心·观光层
attractions.tip: World's 4th tallest building at 599m. 'Free Sky' observation deck at 116th floor. On a clear day you can see Hong Kong's New Territories. Nicknamed '春笋' (bamboo shoot) by locals. Go evening for the best city lights. Book online — walk-up queue is brutal.
attractions.elderly_note: Full elevator access. Suitable for elderly.

attractions.name: OCT Loft Creative Culture Park
attractions.tip: Former factory district turned creative enclave. The abandoned aircraft inside (刷爆小红书的废弃飞机) went viral on 小红书 — it's a real decommissioned aircraft on-site used as an art installation. Galleries, cool indie cafes, street art, vintage markets. Called 'a hipster's paradise.'
attractions.elderly_note: Mostly flat. Some cobblestone between buildings.

attractions.name: Dafen Oil Painting Village
attractions.tip: Village that once produced 60% of the world's oil paintings. Artists now making original work alongside copies. Get a custom oil portrait for ¥200–500. The alleyways now have trendy cafes and galleries alongside the studios. Very photogenic.
attractions.elderly_note: Some uneven alley surfaces. Otherwise accessible.

attractions.name: Lianhuashan Park
attractions.tip: Bronze Deng Xiaoping statue at the top is a pilgrimage for mainland Chinese visitors. The panoramic Shenzhen skyline from the hill is perfectly aligned with the Civic Center skyscrapers — iconic shot. Described as 'a hidden gem — hard to believe it's in the middle of the city.' Spotlessly maintained.
attractions.elderly_note: Gentle incline to top. Benches throughout.

attractions.name: Nantou Ancient Town
attractions.tip: 1,700-year-old walled city revitalized since 2019. Where the Treaty of Nanjing was signed inside — both Shenzhen and Hong Kong trace their administrative roots here. Now: red brick lanes, craft coffee shops, indie boutiques, art exhibitions, weekend pop-up markets. Best described: 'ancient China meets Brooklyn.' Open daily 10am–8pm, closed Mondays.
attractions.elderly_note: Mostly flat cobblestone lanes.

attractions.name: Dameisha Beach
attractions.tip: 1.8km of golden sand backed by green hills — free entry, genuinely beautiful. Best beach in mainland China near a major city. Parasol and jet ski rentals available. Go weekday morning — weekend crowds are enormous. Metro Line 8 to Dameisha Station.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat beach. Suitable with care on sand.

attractions.name: Yantian Coastal Boardwalk
attractions.tip: 19.5km coastal greenway — Shenzhen's most beautiful coastal walk. Connects Shatoujiao, Yantian Harbour, Dameisha to Xiaomeisha. Rocky sea views, lighthouse at Beizai Corner, 'Fairy Tale Town' photo spot. Sunrise is spectacular. Cycle or walk.
attractions.elderly_note: Long route — do only sections. Metro access at multiple points.

attractions.name: Dongmen Pedestrian Street
attractions.tip: Shenzhen's oldest shopping street — bulk fashion, electronics, street food. The fresh-pounded lemon tea (手打柠檬茶) stalls here are a Shenzhen institution. White Rabbit sweet variations in multiple flavors at corner stalls. BBQ skewers everywhere. Evening atmosphere is chaotic and fun.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat pedestrian street.

attractions.name: Window of the World
attractions.tip: 130 miniature world landmarks. Sunday night at 9pm: 1,500-drone light show above the Arch of Triumph — cyberpunk patterns. Robot interactions near major landmarks weekends 2–4pm. Best for families and kids. Evening has more atmosphere than daytime.
attractions.elderly_note: Tram transport within park. Suitable.

attractions.name: Dapeng Peninsula & Ancient City
attractions.tip: Eastern peninsula: speed boats, clifftop hikes, fresh seafood fishing villages. Dapeng Ancient City (大鹏所城) is a 600-year-old Ming dynasty fortress — well-preserved, less touristy. Combine with Judiaosha Beach (角场尾) and Yangmeikeng (杨梅坑 — the bluest water in Shenzhen) for a full day.
attractions.elderly_note: Not suitable due to distance and terrain.

attractions.name: Sea World (Shekou)
attractions.tip: Expat enclave with Western-style bars and restaurants built around a grounded ship (Minghu). The adjacent Sea World Culture and Arts Center is an excellent contemporary art museum — free on some days. Easy access to Hong Kong ferry from here.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat area. Suitable.

attractions.name: Shenzhen Bay Park
attractions.tip: 6.6km seafront greenway along Shenzhen Bay — bike, walk, or rollerskate. Sunset views of the bay and Shenzhen Bay Bridge are excellent. Migratory birds (Oct–March) including rare black-faced spoonbills. Best evening city walk in Shenzhen. Metro Line 9 to Shenzhen Bay Park Station Exit D2.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat paved path. Ideal for elderly.

attractions.name: Huaqiangbei Electronics Market
attractions.tip: China's electronics wholesale capital — phone parts, gadgets, robotics, surveillance systems, drones. SEG E-Market and Huaqiangbei Road have floor after floor of component stalls. If you like tech, this is mesmerizing. Don't buy without knowing prices first. Metro: Huaqiang North Station.
attractions.elderly_note: Busy and crowded. Not recommended for elderly.

attractions.name: Gangtang Hakka Ancient Town
attractions.tip: Called 'Shenzhen's 梦华录' (Dream of Splendor). Hakka culture village with 350-year history — ancient watchtowers, traditional houses, manga museum, ceramics workshop, and Hakka food. Rent hanfu (汉服) from ¥40 for photos. Weekday recommended. Metro Line 10 to Gangtang Station, then community bus.
attractions.elderly_note: Some uneven paths. Mostly accessible.

attractions.name: Zhong-Ying Street (Chung Ying Street)
attractions.tip: Historically unique: one side is Shenzhen, the other is Hong Kong (Sha Tau Kok). 4-meter-wide street, two systems. Old shopfront signs in both Simplified and Traditional Chinese. Requires ID registration to enter. Historic for 'one country two systems' photography. Not busy — almost no tourists know it.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat street. Easy walking.

attractions.name: OCT Harbour & Bay Glory Ferris Wheel
attractions.chinese: 欢乐港湾·湾区之光摩天轮
attractions.tip: Called '湾区之光' (Light of the Bay Area) — one of China's largest sea-view Ferris wheels with transparent gondolas. 360-degree view: Shenzhen Bay, Qianhai, Hong Kong on clear days. Photo spot at the LOVE light sign at base. Best 30 minutes after sunset when city lights come on. Book online.
attractions.elderly_note: Gondola entry requires stepping. Otherwise suitable.

attractions.name: Talangshan Country Park (Sunset Hike)
attractions.tip: 1-hour hike to summit — panoramic Shenzhen city and bay views. Perfect for sunset: start at 6pm, reach top at 7pm as city lights emerge. Metro Line 5 to Tanglang Station. Bring hiking shoes and water. Less crowded than other Shenzhen hills.
attractions.elderly_note: Steep hill. Not suitable for elderly.

attractions.name: Splendid China Folk Village
attractions.tip: Miniature reproductions of 82 China landmarks (Great Wall, Forbidden City, Potala Palace, Terracotta Warriors) at 1:15 scale plus 27 ethnic minority villages. Evening light shows. Good for one comprehensive China culture overview before visiting real sites.
attractions.elderly_note: Large park — tram available. Good for elderly.

attractions.name: Shenzhen Museum
attractions.tip: Tells Shenzhen's incredible story from a 1970s fishing village to China's Silicon Valley in 40 years. Free entry — book via WeChat in advance. The history exhibition is genuinely fascinating: includes exhibits on the first people to 'jump into the sea' (下海) to start businesses after Deng Xiaoping's reform.
attractions.elderly_note: Fully accessible museum.

attractions.name: Shenzhen Happy Valley Theme Park
attractions.tip: 86-acre theme park with 100+ rides. China's first suspended roller coaster (Xueshan Feilong). Playa Maya Water Park inside, open May–early October. Night shows on weekends. Best for families with teens.
attractions.elderly_note: Not suitable for most elderly — rides are intense.

food.name: Dim Sum at Fanlou
food.where: Fanlou 蘩楼 — Futian District, multiple locations. Famous spot always with queues.
food.tip: Arrive before 10am to get a number. XLB (小笼包), siu mai (烧卖), and shrimp dumplings (虾饺) are standouts. Check Dianping for voucher deals — usually 80元 for 100元 worth. The cha siu bao (叉烧包) here is exceptional.

food.name: Taier Sauerkraut Fish
food.where: Taier 太二 — multiple Shenzhen locations (CoCo Park, Coastal City, etc.)
food.tip: Famous Shenzhen-origin chain known for historic 4-hour queues. Use WeChat/app remote queue while exploring nearby. One fish, one pot, one sauce. The sauerkraut broth is genuinely unlike fish hotpot anywhere else. The restaurant's cheeky rule: 'No modifications to the menu.'

food.name: BBQ Skewers at CoCo Park
food.where: 西塔老太太泥炉烤肉 at CoCo Park, Futian — popular Friday night local hangout
food.tip: The grilled eel (烤鳗鱼) here is extraordinary — smoky sweetness unlike anything in Singapore. Lemon8 SG travellers repeatedly call it a 'taste you can't find back home.' Also order: enoki mushrooms with chili oil (金针菇), lamb skewers (羊肉串).

food.name: Fermented Rice Wine Milk Tea
food.where: 阿嬷手作 (Grandma Handmade) — Futian area multiple locations
food.tip: Exclusive to Shenzhen/GZ PRD region. Fermented rice wine base with taro balls and milk tea cream — complex, slightly tart, unlike anything in Singapore. Multiple Lemon8 reviewers call it their single best drink discovery in China.

food.name: Spicy Sour Glass Noodles
food.where: 连城新天地 (Liancheng Xintianye) mall food court
food.tip: Thick potato starch noodles in a spicy-sour broth — 中辣 (medium) unless you're confident. At ¥9.90 a bowl, best value eat in Shenzhen. Completely different texture from Singapore's instant version.

food.name: Crab Roe Pasta (Trending Fusion)
food.where: Gentle Pasta 健意 — PA Mall, Futian
food.tip: Italian pasta with Chinese crab roe sauce — trending hard on 小红书 and Lemon8. Weekend wait is about 30 mins. The signature crab roe pasta is non-negotiable order. Unusual fusion that works remarkably well.

food.name: Egg Tarts (HK Style)
food.where: HK-style cha chaan teng cafes throughout Shenzhen; Sudaxing bakery
food.tip: Shenzhen's proximity to HK means exceptional Hong Kong-style egg tarts everywhere. Multi-layered flaky pastry, creamy custard. Eat fresh out of the oven. Significantly better and cheaper than Singapore versions.

food.name: Claypot Rice (Random Local Shop)
food.where: Any local 煲仔饭 shop in Futian or Luohu neighborhoods — look for the clay pots on open stoves
food.tip: In Shenzhen, unlike GZ, good claypot rice isn't at famous shops — it's the random neighborhood place. Multiple Lemon8 reviews: 'went into a random place in Futian, staff so friendly, food so good and cheap.' Look for smoke coming from the pots.

food.name: Potchuang Hakka Food at Gangtang
food.where: Restaurants inside Gangtang Hakka Town 甘坑客家小镇
food.tip: Hakka cuisine: 酿豆腐 (stuffed tofu with pork — Hakka's most famous dish), 猪肚鸡 (pork stomach and chicken soup with white pepper), 客家黄酒 (fermented rice wine). Earthy, home-style, deeply traditional. Very different from Cantonese.

food.name: Late Night Congee Street (Sandpot Congee)
food.where: 卖鱼佬砂锅粥 (Luohu) — Dianping 2024 Must-Eat Award winner. 牛巷番薯粥 — Dianping Top 1 small eats.
food.tip: Shenzhen's late-night supper culture is centered around slow-cooked sandpot congee. Unlike Guangzhou's boat congee, this version simmers longer and is served in hot clay pots. Seafood, pork, fish — order the freshest item of the day. Best after 9pm.

food.name: Hand-Pounded Lemon Tea
food.where: Stalls throughout Dongmen and Futian areas — look for live pounding action
food.tip: The lemon tea here is fresh-pounded with actual whole lemons bashed against the cup to release oils from the skin. Completely different from Singapore's bottled lemon tea. Always order 微糖 (low sugar). A Shenzhen institution.

food.name: Braised Duck & Chicken Feet
food.where: Dongmen area stalls; Shuiwei Night Market, Futian
food.tip: Shenzhen's brine-braised meats are exceptional — braised duck with aromatic spice broth, chicken feet in mala (spicy numbing) or lemon flavor. The Shuiwei Night Market in Futian is the best night food destination —琳琅满目的小吃 (endless variety of local snacks).

food.name: Chagee (Tea) — Original
food.where: Multiple locations throughout Shenzhen
food.tip: Chagee originated in Yunnan and exploded across China. In Shenzhen it's ¥18–25 vs Singapore's ¥7–10 premium. The TGTC (Tie Guan Yin milk tea with cheese cream) here uses actual tieguanyin — different product to what Singapore gets. Worth the comparison.

food.name: Omakase at Sushi Jyukan
food.where: Sushi Jyukan — Futian District (search on Gaode Maps)
food.tip: Premium omakase with foie gras and sea urchin for ~SGD$56 — viral on Lemon8 SG as the best value omakase outside Japan. The quality-to-price ratio shocked multiple Singapore reviewers. Booking essential — WeChat only.

practical.payment: Alipay and WeChat Pay. Link YouTrip or Trust Bank card for fee-free CNY payments. ¥200 cash for markets.
practical.transport: 17-line metro — very efficient and bilingual. High-speed rail to Guangzhou takes 30–40 mins from Shenzhen North (深圳北). Easy day trip between cities.
practical.from_hk: High-speed rail from West Kowloon to Futian: 13 minutes. Lo Wu/Lok Ma Chau border crossing also easy. Ferry from Shekou to Hong Kong Pier: 30 mins.
practical.connectivity: VPN essential before arrival. Airalo eSIM recommended. Download DiDi for taxis.
practical.best_time: October–December. Avoid summer (extremely hot and humid). November–December best weather.
practical.booking_apps: Dianping for restaurant discovery and vouchers. Klook for Window of the World, Happy Valley, theme parks. WeChat for most local bookings.
practical.book_ahead: Ping An observation deck online. Window of the World drone show — Sunday 9pm. Gangtang recommended weekdays.

shanghai.name: Shanghai
shanghai.best_time: March–May (spring) and September–November (autumn). November is peak season from SG — golden foliage.

attractions.name: The Bund
attractions.tip: 52 historic buildings in Gothic, Baroque, Art Deco styles — the 'Museum of 10,000 Nations.' Best photo position: 九江路 (Jiujiang Road) for the full sweep. Evening light show from May–October. Don't miss at 23:00 — lights turn off sharp. Walk to Waibaidu Bridge (外白渡桥) for classic Old Shanghai shots.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat riverside promenade. Excellent for elderly.

attractions.name: Tianzifang
attractions.tip: 石库门 Shikumen stone-gate architecture converted to creative lanes. Get there by 10am before tour groups. The real value is the side alleys — Tianzifang alleys get the tourist traffic, but duck into the smaller 弄堂 (longtang) side streets for the authentic lanes. Metro Line 9: Dapuqiao Station.
attractions.elderly_note: Some narrow alleys. Mostly flat.

attractions.name: Yu Garden (Yuyuan)
attractions.tip: 450-year-old Ming dynasty garden. The 玉玲珑 (Yu Linglong) rock alongside Su Zhou's Guan Yun Peak and Hangzhou's Zhou Yun Peak are the 'Three Great Taihu Rocks of the South.' The surrounding commercial bazaar: eat at Nanxiang Steamed Bun Restaurant for XLB at the 九曲桥 (Nine-Bend Bridge). Beautiful lit up at night.
attractions.elderly_note: Some steps within garden. Overall accessible.

attractions.name: Wukang Road & French Concession
attractions.tip: 14 heritage buildings along Wukang Road including Wukang Building (武康大楼), Ba Jin's former residence, Song Qingling's former home. The full walk: Wukang Road → Anfu Road → Fumin Road → Changshu Road. The %Arabica coffee opposite Wukang Building's curved face is the best photo spot. Go before noon — afternoon gets very crowded.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat tree-lined streets. Ideal for leisurely walks.

attractions.name: Xintiandi
attractions.tip: Shikumen architecture preserved as upscale restaurant and retail district. Navigate to 'Xintiandi North' (新天地北里) for the best section. Also contains Site of First National Congress of the Communist Party — historic and free to visit. Walk to adjacent Sinan Mansions (思南公馆) for gorgeous garden villas.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat heritage streets. Good for elderly.

attractions.name: Shanghai Tower Sky Observatory
attractions.chinese: 上海中心大厦·上海之巅
attractions.tip: World's fastest elevator. 119th floor — 2nd highest observation deck globally. FREE HACK: Go to B1 level entrance of Shanghai Tower — there's a free panoramic viewing area inside the lobby that most tourists don't know about. Actual paid deck is ¥180 but worth it for the best views. See all 3 Lujiazui towers from above.
attractions.elderly_note: Full elevator access. Suitable for elderly.

attractions.name: Nanjing Road Pedestrian Street
attractions.tip: Look for the HKRI Taikoo Hui mall on Nanjing Road — inside is the TikTok/小红书-famous curved dragon escalator (龙形自动扶梯). The mall's B1 food hall has excellent local eats. At night the whole street glows with neon — classic Shanghai energy.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat pedestrian street. Very accessible.

attractions.name: Zhujiajiao Water Town
attractions.tip: Ancient water town 1 hr from city — gondola rides (¥80/boat), 36 stone bridges, riverside Ming-Qing architecture. 'Nine turns and eighteen bends' waterway. Go weekday — weekends are packed. Take Metro Line 17 to Zhujiajiao Station. Combine with Qingpu for a full half-day trip.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat canal paths. Gondola is accessible with assistance.

attractions.name: 1933 Old Millfun
attractions.tip: Former 1933 slaughterhouse — Art Deco meets Bauhaus, outer square inner circle, concrete spiral ramps wide enough for cattle, interlocking sky bridges. Now galleries, cafes, restaurants, art events. Called 'a light and shadow maze of industrial heritage.' Few tourists know it. Art photography lovers will obsess over this building.
attractions.elderly_note: Some ramps and stairs. Partially accessible.

attractions.name: M50 Art District
attractions.tip: 120+ contemporary art galleries in a converted Suzhou Creek textile mill. Shanghai's real underground art scene. Free entry to most spaces. Affordable original art prints available. Combine with a walk along Suzhou Creek riverside for a quiet, unhurried Shanghai afternoon.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat factory compound. Accessible.

attractions.name: Shanghai Museum (People's Square)
attractions.chinese: 上海博物馆·人民广场馆
attractions.tip: One of China's four great museums — nearly 1 million artefacts across 21 categories: bronze, ceramics, calligraphy, jade, painting, coin. Free but requires WeChat advance booking (People's Square branch). East branch (浦东馆) doesn't require advance booking. Book Tuesday–Sunday 9am–5pm. Closed Mondays.
attractions.elderly_note: Fully accessible. Elevator throughout.

attractions.name: Jing'an Temple & District
attractions.tip: Active Buddhist temple sandwiched between luxury malls — gold roof, incense, monks amidst skyscrapers. The juxtaposition is uniquely Shanghai. The surrounding Jing'an district is Shanghai's most livable neighborhood — good cafes, Jade Buddha Temple nearby (free), international vibe. Metro Line 2/7: Jing'an Temple Station.
attractions.elderly_note: Some steps at temple entrance. Surrounding streets flat.

attractions.name: Sinan Mansions (Sinan Road)
attractions.tip: Former French Concession garden villas housing high-end restaurants and boutiques. Plane trees lining the streets create a natural canopy. A quieter, more refined alternative to Xintiandi. The old Song Qingling residence and Zhou Enlai's former home are nearby. Best on a sunny afternoon.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat streets. Ideal for gentle walks.

attractions.name: Suzhou Creek Walking Route
attractions.tip: Locals call this Shanghai's best-kept secret walking route — far less crowded than Bund or Wukang Road. The riverbanks from Changhua Road to Suzhou Creek are lined with restored warehouse galleries and independent coffee shops. Morning mist on the creek is atmospheric. Better Citywalk than Wukang Road for avoiding crowds.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat riverside path. Excellent for elderly.

attractions.name: Shanghai Disney Resort
attractions.tip: TRON Lightcycle Run is the main unique ride (not at other Disneys). Buy tickets with Premier Access for the big rides — worth the premium on weekends. New spring festival light shows in 2025 — fireworks at 20:15 and 21:30. Book minimum 1 week ahead. Metro Line 11 direct from city.
attractions.elderly_note: Large park — wheelchair rental available. Suitable with mobility aids.

attractions.name: Longhua Temple
attractions.tip: Shanghai's oldest and largest Buddhist temple, founded 242 AD. Adjacent Longhua Martyrs' Cemetery is a peaceful memorial park. Much less visited than Jing'an — the authentic temple experience without the crowds. March–April: peach blossom season turns the temple grounds spectacular.
attractions.elderly_note: Mostly flat. Some steps. Suitable.

attractions.name: Tiantian Love Road (Sweet Love Road)
attractions.tip: Called 'Shanghai's most romantic road.' The love letter postbox at the entrance stamps every letter sent with a special 'love' postmark. Couples queue to write notes and mail them. Wall of love poetry murals. Close to Lu Xun Museum and 1933 Old Millfun — good half-day area.
attractions.elderly_note: Short flat road. Accessible.

attractions.name: West Bund Art Zone
attractions.tip: Shanghai's answer to London's South Bank — contemporary art museums (Long Museum, TANK Shanghai) in converted oil tanks along Huangpu River. Free to walk the riverbank. TANK Shanghai's entrance fee is worth it for temporary exhibitions. Beautiful at dusk with sunset over the river. Metro Line 11: Yunzhong Road Station.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat riverside paths. Accessible.

attractions.name: People's Square & Historical Museum
attractions.chinese: 人民广场·上海历史博物馆
attractions.tip: The Historical Museum (上海历史博物馆) inside the old Hong Kong Shanghai Racing Club building is free and excellent. Tells Shanghai's complete story from fishing village to global megacity. People's Square itself has: Lego flagship store, M&M flagship, Madame Tussauds, Shanghai Museum — whole complex is a full day.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat plaza. Excellent for elderly.

attractions.name: Qibao Ancient Town
attractions.tip: Less touristy than Zhujiajiao — smaller but more authentic. Ancient street food along the canal: rice cakes, glutinous rice, smelly tofu, sugar-cane juice. Good weekday morning outing. Metro Line 9 to Qibao Station (20 mins from city center). Better food experience than Zhujiajiao at lower cost.
attractions.elderly_note: Mostly flat. Accessible.

attractions.name: Shanghai Greenhouse Garden
attractions.tip: Described as 'more Studio Ghibli than city centre.' Botanical glasshouse where art, tropical plants, and architecture converge. Winding paths, sun-dappled corners, excellent photo spots. Very popular on 小红书 for couple photography. Book ahead — weekends sell out.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat paths within greenhouse. Suitable.

attractions.name: Duolun Road Cultural Street
attractions.tip: 550-meter pedestrian street dedicated to early 20th century Chinese literary figures. Lu Xun's former home and museum nearby. Bronze statues of writers, antique shops, old teahouses. China's literary history made physical. Combine with 1933 Old Millfun and Sweet Love Road for a full Hongkou cultural day.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat pedestrian street. Suitable.

attractions.name: Oriental Pearl Tower & Riverfront
attractions.tip: 468m tall, iconic pink globe towers. The glass floor viewing platform is the must-do at the 263m Sightseeing Sphere. The Jin Mao Tower 88th floor 'Walk in the Clouds' skywalk is scarier and more unique. The Shanghai History Museum inside Pearl Tower is included in ticket price — actually excellent.
attractions.elderly_note: Full elevator access. Suitable.

attractions.name: Xinchang Ancient Town
attractions.tip: Called Shanghai's best-kept water town secret — even locals don't all know it. Film location for Ang Lee's 色戒 (Lust, Caution). Traditional teahouses, Old Eight Styles local dishes (老八样), almost no foreign tourists. Metro Line 16 to Xinchang Station. Different feel from Zhujiajiao — quieter, rawer.
attractions.elderly_note: Flat canal streets. Accessible.

attractions.name: Jiahe Food Market
attractions.tip: A 小红书-discovered local wet market that became a pilgrimage for food lovers. 20–30 mins from city center. Specializes in fresh vegetables and unusual ingredients. The market has multiple small eateries attached — eat where you literally shop. Less crowded, completely authentic.
attractions.elderly_note: Busy indoor market. Some uneven surfaces.

food.name: Xiao Long Bao (Soup Dumplings)
food.where: Jia Jia Tang Bao 佳家汤包 — Huanghe Road No.90 (local institution, always queues); Nanxiang Mantou Dian 南翔馒头店 in Yu Garden (est. 1900, birthplace of XLB); Lai Lai XLB 莱莱小笼 (viral on 小红书, Michelin-noted)
food.tip: Technique: bite small hole at side → sip soup → eat. The non-oily broth at Jia Jia Tang Bao is the gold standard. Lai Lai XLB's crab roe version (蟹黄小笼包) is the premium option. Nanxiang is the original but more tourist-heavy. 佳家 wins on purity of flavour.

food.name: Sheng Jian Bao (Pan-Fried Pork Buns)
food.where: Yang's Fried Dumplings 小杨生煎 (multiple locations, eat standing up fresh from pan); Da Hu Chun 大壶春 (charcoal pan method — smoky and more traditional)
food.tip: Shanghai's most iconic street breakfast. Crispy golden bottom, juicy pork and soup inside, soft steamed top. Eat immediately — crust softens fast. Locals eat standing outside the shop. The charcoal-pan version at Da Hu Chun has smokier flavor. ¥14.5 for 4 pieces at Da Hu Chun.

food.name: Scallion Oil Noodles
food.where: Any local noodle shop — look for 'congyu ban mian' on menu. Tianzifang area stalls.
food.tip: Brown noodles tossed in rendered scallion oil with soy sauce — looks plain, tastes transformative. The caramelized scallion oil is the key. Pure Shanghai home cooking. One of those dishes that proves perfect simplicity.

food.name: Hong Shao Rou (Red Braised Pork Belly)
food.where: Old Jesse 老吉士 (No.41 Tianping Road, Xuhui — local favourite since forever); Xing'an Restaurant (Xin'an Road, only 8 tables — lines up early)
food.tip: Shanghai's comfort food soul — thick pork belly slow-braised in soy sauce, sugar, Shaoxing wine until melt-in-mouth and glazed dark. Shanghai version is distinctly sweeter than other regions. Eat with plain white rice — the sauce absorbed into rice is the point. Old Jesse does the definitive version.

food.name: Crab Roe Dishes (Seasonal)
food.where: Restaurants throughout Huaihai Road; Xie Huang Yu 蟹黄鱼 (Taicang Road No.200) for yellow croaker noodle soup with crab roe
food.tip: October–December: hairy crab (大闸蟹) season. Everything gets crab roe: XLB, noodles, tofu, pasta, rice. The full steamed hairy crab at Wang Bao He (王宝和) restaurant near People's Square is the traditional experience — order the set to include crab paste dumplings. Non-negotiable if visiting in season.

food.name: You Tiao & Soy Milk Breakfast
food.where: Morning street stalls throughout residential areas near your hotel
food.tip: The most authentic Shanghai morning: fried dough stick (油条) with warm soy milk (豆浆). Locals order salty soy milk (咸豆浆 — with vinegar and spring onion that curdles the soy milk into silky pudding). Find a neighborhood stall, not a franchise. ¥8 for a complete breakfast.

food.name: Charcoal Hotpot (Hidden Gem)
food.where: 1100 Dingxi Road, Changning District — old-school charcoal hotpot. Open Mon–Fri 11am–2pm, 4:30pm–2am; Sat–Sun 11am–2am
food.tip: Soup simmered over real charcoal fire — broth from ¥28. Old Shanghai charm with unbeatable prices. The broths start thin and deepen over the meal. YouTrip SG calls it 'a warm hug but a fraction of Singapore prices.' Very local crowd.

food.name: Jianbing (Savory Breakfast Crepe)
food.where: Morning street stalls, available until 11am only. Near residential areas and near Jia Jia Tang Bao on Huanghe Road.
food.tip: Paper-thin crepe spread on a hot griddle, egg cracked and spread, then filled with crispy crackers, scallions, pickled vegetables, hoisin sauce. The Shandong-style jianbing in Shanghai is different from Beijing's — crisper, lighter. Order with extra 脆饼 (crispy cracker) inside.

food.name: Soufflé Pancake
food.where: Trending cafes along Anfu Road and Wukang Road area
food.tip: Japanese-style jiggly soufflé pancakes are a 小红书 staple in Shanghai — multiple flavors (matcha, original, seasonal). Very photogenic — made for the gram. Best on weekday mornings to avoid 30-min waits. The matcha version at quality cafes is consistently excellent.

food.name: Hogwarts Bakery (Apoli Itabakery)
food.where: Apoli Itabakery — Changning District. Search Gaode for exact location.
food.tip: Arched windows, warm amber lighting, stained glass — wizard-school aesthetic. The croissants and pastries are genuinely excellent (not just Instagram bait). Blew up on 小红书. Located between Tianzifang and French Concession area — perfect afternoon stop.

food.name: Shanghai Drunken Chicken
food.where: Jesse Restaurant 老吉士 (Tianping Road); Fu 1039 (Yuyuan Road, Changning) — upscale version
food.tip: Cold chicken marinated in Shaoxing wine, goji berries, ginger. Served in jelly-set wine broth. Distinctly Shanghai: herbal, alcoholic, refreshing. Perfect summer dish. The wine-infused quality should be powerful — if it tastes mild, it's been cut. Order as starter before heavier dishes.

food.name: Matcha Gelato at Kujuye
food.where: 九十葉 — Anfu Road / French Concession area
food.tip: 5 grades of matcha with gelato and drink options — the difference between the grades is genuinely perceptible. The premium matcha (ceremonial grade) gelato is extraordinary. Went viral on 小红书 and repeatedly cited by Lemon8 SG reviewers. Small queue but worth it.

food.name: Pain Chaud Bakery
food.where: 百丘 Pain Chaud — multiple locations, Paris Baguette-style local franchise
food.tip: Shanghai's beloved local bakery chain — coffee, ice cream, breads, pastries, quiche. The caramel croissant is the signature. Multiple reviewers cite this as better than franchise competitors at lower price. 2025: expanding rapidly, now over 40 locations.

food.name: Songshu Guiyu (Squirrel Fish)
food.where: Fu 1088 (Zhenning Road) — upscale Shanghainese; Zhangshengji (LuJiaZui West Road) for premium version
food.tip: Whole mandarin fish deboned, scored, deep-fried until porcupine-shaped, topped with bright red sweet-and-sour sauce. Visually stunning — the fish fans out like a squirrel tail. Festive dish traditionally for special occasions. The crispy-to-tender texture contrast is exceptional. Order as main sharing dish.

food.name: Pork Ribs & Rice Cake
food.where: Xian De Lai 鲜得来 (Yunnan South Road) — traditional Shanghai specialty restaurant
food.tip: Crispy fried pork ribs served with soft, chewy rice cakes in special sauce — a pairing unique to Shanghai. Xian De Lai has been making this since the 1940s. The rice cake (年糕) absorbs the sauce perfectly. Very cheap, very local, very satisfying.

practical.payment: Alipay and WeChat Pay everywhere. Link YouTrip, Wise, or Trust Bank card for best exchange rates. ¥200 cash for taxi tips and small stalls.
practical.transport: Metro is the best way to get around — comprehensive, bilingual, efficient. DiDi for taxis. AMap (高德地图) for navigation.
practical.connectivity: Airalo eSIM before flying. VPN essential — install before leaving Singapore. China Unicom or China Mobile SIM available at Pudong Airport.
practical.language: Most tourist areas have English signage. More English-friendly than Guangzhou and Shenzhen.
practical.booking_apps: Dianping for restaurant vouchers and discovery. Klook for Disney, Pearl Tower. WeChat mini-programmes for museum bookings. Meituan for food delivery.
practical.book_ahead: Shanghai Disney — 1 week ahead minimum, especially weekends. Shanghai Museum (People's Square branch) — WeChat advance booking. Shanghai Greenhouse Garden — book online. Zhujiajiao — weekday strongly recommended.
practical.best_time: March–May (spring) and September–November (autumn). November is peak from SG — beautiful plane tree foliage on Wukang Road and French Concession. December–February: cold but cheaper and less crowded.
practical.day_trips: Suzhou (苏州, 25 min by high-speed rail) — classical gardens, silk market. Hangzhou (杭州, 45 min) — West Lake. Both are easy day trips from Shanghai.
practical.transport_tip: 24-hour public transport card available at Metro customer service — convenient for multi-day stays.
